analysis.core_story_summary: Sutji, once narrated the following tale to the assembled sages which sheds light on the origin of jewels and other precious stones - ' During ancient times there lived a mighty demon named Bala. Although he had defeated the deities and driven them out from the heaven, yet he had assured them that he would extend fullest cooperation and support in any yagya performed by deities in future. All the deities planned to perform a 'Yagya' in order to regain their lost kingdom.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.
Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **How Jewels Originated — Kids (8-12):** Sutji, once narrated the following tale to the assembled sages which sheds light on the origin of jewels and other precious stones - ' During ancient times there lived a mighty demon named Bala. Although he had defeated the deities and driven them out from the heaven, yet he had assured them that he would extend fullest cooperation and support in any yagya performed by deities in future. All the deities planned to perform a 'Yagya' in order to regain their lost kingdom. This story teaches the cost of conflict. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **How Jewels Originated — Teens (13-17):** Sutji, once narrated the following tale to the assembled sages which sheds light on the origin of jewels and other precious stones - ' During ancient times there lived a mighty demon named Bala. Although he had defeated the deities and driven them out from the heaven, yet he had assured them that he would extend fullest cooperation and support in any yagya performed by deities in future. All the deities planned to perform a 'Yagya' in order to regain their lost kingdom. For teens, the key idea is the cost of conflict. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **How Jewels Originated — Young Adults:** Sutji, once narrated the following tale to the assembled sages which sheds light on the origin of jewels and other precious stones - ' During ancient times there lived a mighty demon named Bala. Although he had defeated the deities and driven them out from the heaven, yet he had assured them that he would extend fullest cooperation and support in any yagya performed by deities in future. All the deities planned to perform a 'Yagya' in order to regain their lost kingdom. A modern takeaway is the cost of conflict. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **How Jewels Originated — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** Sutji, once narrated the following tale to the assembled sages which sheds light on the origin of jewels and other precious stones - ' During ancient times there lived a mighty demon named Bala. Although he had defeated the deities and driven them out from the heaven, yet he had assured them that he would extend fullest cooperation and support in any yagya performed by deities in future. All the deities planned to perform a 'Yagya' in order to regain their lost kingdom. The episode highlights the cost of conflict. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **How Jewels Originated — Global General Audience:** Sutji, once narrated the following tale to the assembled sages which sheds light on the origin of jewels and other precious stones - ' During ancient times there lived a mighty demon named Bala. Although he had defeated the deities and driven them out from the heaven, yet he had assured them that he would extend fullest cooperation and support in any yagya performed by deities in future. All the deities planned to perform a 'Yagya' in order to regain their lost kingdom. In universal terms, it explores the cost of conflict. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
